summary: Verified /app/render.yaml fixes against /app/backend/server.py. All 8 file-integrity sub-checks (1a-1h) PASS. All 4 startup-command checks (2a-2d) PASS. Runtime health, catalog, and admin login all return 200 with expected payloads. Every env key referenced in server.py is present in render.yaml; no orphan env keys. Note: request stated /api/catalog/symptoms is a 'public read' but it actually requires auth (Depends(get_current_user)) — this is not a regression, just a mis-stated assumption; endpoint returns 200 when called with a valid Bearer token.

1_file_integrity.1g_missing_env_keys_referenced_by_server: PASS — none missing. server.py references: MONGO_URL, DB_NAME, RESEND_API_KEY, SENDER_EMAIL, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_PAYMENT_LINK, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, SUBSCRIPTION_PRICE_USD, SUBSCRIPTION_TRIAL_DAYS, JWT_SECRET, EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL, ADMIN_EMAIL, ADMIN_PASSWORD, ADMIN_USERNAME (14 total). All 14 are declared in render.yaml.
1_file_integrity.1h_orphan_env_keys_in_render_yaml: PASS — none. All 14 render.yaml envVars entries are consumed by server.py. No Render-only extras (PORT is provided by Render platform automatically and referenced in startCommand).

validations.3_runtime_health: PASS — GET http://localhost:8001/api/health returned 200 with body {"ok":true,"service":"mindtrack-api"} (exact match).
validations.4_regression_catalog_symptoms: PASS (with clarification) — endpoint requires auth (Depends(get_current_user)); returned 401 without token, 200 with valid admin Bearer token. Server routing intact.
validations.5_auth_login: PASS — POST /api/auth/login with admin credentials returned 200 with access_token (JWT) and user.role='admin'.

action_items: Optional (not a bug): Task description called /api/catalog/symptoms 'public read', but server.py:522 protects it with Depends(get_current_user). If the intent is truly public, remove the dependency; otherwise update the description.
Optional cleanup: render.yaml still contains an inline comment (line 44) suggesting to change SENDER_EMAIL after Resend domain verification — documentation only, no code change required.
critical_code_review_comments: render.yaml env vars now exactly match server.py — no drift.
SUBSCRIPTION_PRICE_USD and SUBSCRIPTION_TRIAL_DAYS values are quoted strings ('1.99', '7'); server.py handles the int() cast for TRIAL_DAYS and keeps PRICE_USD as string — safe.
JWT_SECRET uses generateValue:true — good, no hardcoded secret.
context_for_next_testing_agent: render.yaml is now consistent with backend/server.py env usage. Admin login credentials in /app/memory/test_credentials.md (admin@mindtrack.app / Admin@12345) work against local backend on :8001. Backend service is healthy and reachable at /api/health.
rca_of_issue: Original bug: render.yaml drifted from server.py — used old key names (RESEND_FROM_EMAIL, STRIPE_PRICE_ID), missed subscription config keys, contained an unused EMERGENT_LLM_KEY, and pointed EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL at the root domain. Fix applied by main agent renamed/added/removed keys and set EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL to the api. subdomain. Verified by 1:1 diff of os.environ.get/[...] keys in server.py vs render.yaml envVars list, plus live curl checks on health, login, and catalog endpoints.
